mechanics.breadcrumb_path: Rules Compendium > Page 016 > quests > For example_ ranged attack against moving character would
mechanics.excerpt: For example_ ranged attack against moving character would use the target'$ INT RFL as the base target number; The target'$ Intelligence represents (in part) perceptiveness allowing a Intelligent character to notice attack and source quickly and accurately: Good Reflexes then provide the character the means tO move his body out 0f the way of the shot: is always the gamemaster decide which Attributes are used to generate target numbers in cases where tne rules do not specify them: Always use two different Attributes when generating base target numbers from Attributes sOme cases; the first Attribute willl be obvious while tne second might take more thought; For example_ arm wrestling contest clearly involves the Strength of your opponent; Your first impulse might be t0 simply apply Strength twice_ but instead you should use Body or Willpower as the second Attribute Since represents (among other things} character' endurance, the most likely candidate would be Body: REBOLVING ACTION CHECKS When a Action Check is called for, the gamemaster first establishes the difficulty of the task in the form of base tar- get number: Depending on the situation, certain modifiers may also apply; which may increase or decrease the target number: The task is then resolved by making 2D10 Action Check roll; Skill Check is being made the character'$ appropriate skill bonus added to the dice result: If the total result is equal to greater than the target number; the task succeeds_ If not, fails _ As for the specific results of success and failure_ see Quality of Success; P: 17 , Fumbles Even the best characters fail every now and then: To rep resent this any result of on the Action Check dice (both dice come up 1s) automatically fails_ regardless of the target num- ber or any modifiers_ This failure called tumble and at the gamemaster' option can have entertaining negative effects especially the character was attempting something foolhardy trivial .
